title: CEO Commentary on Shares & Deposits: As of March 31, 2026
content:
Total member shares declined by 1.8% as of March 31, 2026, representing a $1,579 thousand decrease from the prior period. This contraction reflects an overall tightening in share balances across several categories.

Regular Shares remain the largest funding component at 44.0% of total shares, totaling $36,002 thousand ($81,913 thousand). Share Certificates account for $22,285 thousand (27.2%), while Share Drafts total $13,430 thousand (16.4%). Money Market Shares total $5,363 thousand (6.5%) of the funding mix, while IRA/Keogh Accounts represent $4,771 thousand (5.8%). Smaller balances within other share categories remain limited.

Most core deposit categories declined from the previous period, particularly Share Certificates (-9.4%), All Other Shares (-12.2%), and Share Drafts (-1.1%); conversely, Regular Shares grew by 2.3%, while Money Market Shares increased by 2.6%, although these balances remain immaterial to the overall funding structure.

Overall, Formation Credit Union's deposit structure remains well diversified and predominantly composed of core member funding. Management continues to focus on retaining core member relationships, maintaining deposit competitiveness, and supporting long-term funding stability.
